content_hints.description: Level 3 features include the Monastery of the Spirited Leaf.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a stone landing slick with condensation. The air is heavier here, thick with moisture that beads on your skin and the taste of deep minerals—iron, limestone, and something organic beneath it all, like the breath of a subterranean river. Dwarven mason's marks are chiseled into the walls at precise intervals, each one a different hand, each one centuries old.
event_triggers: On entry: The distant sound of rushing water echoes from the south. The Sargauth River.

content_hints.description: The Sargauth River fills a cavern a hundred feet wide, its black water moving with a patient, irresistible current. The surface is glass-smooth and utterly opaque, and a stone quay juts into the water, mooring three flat-bottomed boats.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see the cavern opens wide, and the Sargauth River fills it edge to edge—a hundred feet of black water moving with a slow, irresistible current. The surface is glass-smooth and utterly opaque, swallowing your torchlight without reflection.

hazards.name: River current (Strength save or swept 60 ft downstream)
Deep water (no bottom visible; something large moves below)

event_triggers: On water contact: The river is ice-cold and leaves a faint phosphorescent residue on the skin that takes hours to fade.
On boat launch: The painted eyes on the prow glow faintly as the boat enters the current, then go dark.

content_hints.description: Green Slime covers each 10-foot-wide, 15-foot-high section of vertical wall with six 5-foot-square patches of green slime. This slime obscures a secret door, and ability checks made to search the walls for it are made with disadvantage until the slime is scraped off the section of wall in which the secret door is hidden.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see thick webbing filling the cavern from floor to ceiling, coating every surface in sticky, pale strands that vibrate at the slightest disturbance. The air is close and sweet with decay, and wrapped bundles hang from the ceiling at odd intervals.

loot.name: Potion of Spider Climbing (in web cocoon)

hazards.name: Web strands (restrained on contact, DC 12 to escape)

event_triggers: On rescue attempt: The wrapped figure is a drow scout who will reluctantly provide information about the outpost ahead in exchange for freedom.

content_hints.description: The chamber features plated walls, and at the end of the room is near a structure that collapses, where a creature within half the distance of the structure's height must make a DC 25 Dexterity saving throw. The room is lit by continual flame spells cast on iron torch sconces bolted to the walls, and mounted on stone plaques between the sconces are the heads of three dwarves, two orogs, a troglodyte, and a gargoyle.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a room filled with wooden trestle tables and benches, lit by continual flame spells cast on iron torch sconces bolted to the walls. Mounted on stone plaques between the sconces are the heads of three dwarves, two orogs, a troglodyte, and a gargoyle.

npcs.name: T'rissa Auvryndar
npcs.dialogue: Surface dwellers. How quaint. You have come seeking the deeper levels, yes? Everyone does. Few survive. I will make you an offer: serve House Auvryndar for one task, and I will grant you safe passage to Level 4. Refuse, and you may try to swim. The river is very patient.
npcs.notes: Drow priestess of Lolth, commander of the outpost. Cunning and pragmatic. Will use the party as expendable assets against rival drow houses. Honors deals but interprets them with spider-web precision.

event_triggers: On diplomacy (DC 15 Persuasion): T'rissa offers safe passage through the outpost and information about Level 4 in exchange for clearing the spider nest.

content_hints.description: A large, opulent room features green marble tiling on the floor and walls painted with lifelike forest scenes depicting predators stalking prey. In the center of the tabernacle is a block of blood-stained white marble. Eerie clicking sounds betray the presence of seven hook horrors, which attempt to hook stray creatures.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a large, opulent room with green marble tiling on the floor and walls painted with lifelike forest scenes of predators stalking prey. In the center of the room is an altar made of stained white marble, beside which lies an inanimate human skeleton clad in tattered hide armor. Eerie clicking sounds betray the presence of seven hook horrors lurking in the shadows.

hazards.name: Rune traps (random effects: light, darkness, teleport, alarm)

event_triggers: On crossing: Roll 1d4 for each rune stepped on. 1: Faerie Fire (outlined for 1 minute). 2: Darkness (10-foot radius). 3: Teleported back to start. 4: Alarm (drow alerted).

content_hints.description: The chamber is clearly the workplace of a wizard, featuring open tomes on astronomy and astrology, a massive telescope of brass, crystal, and polished mahogany on an intricate stand, a ladder against one wall, and an immense crystal lens embedded in the wall. A pair of voluminous tomes on astronomy and astrology lie open on a table, and a locked wooden chest about the size of ISKANOER sits in the corner.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step into a chamber where a massive telescope of brass, crystal, and polished mahogany rests on an intricate stand in the middle of the chamber. A ladder leans against one wall, and an immense crystal lens is embedded in the wall.

loot.name: Shield of the Sentinel (+1 Shield, glows near secret doors)

hazards.name: Divine ward (non-dwarves take 2d6 radiant on altar touch)

event_triggers: On dwarf character prayer: Dumathoin grants a vision of a secret passage on this level, revealed for 1 hour.
On desecration attempt: The sealed face on the altar opens its eyes. The temperature drops thirty degrees in an instant.

content_hints.description: Altonrel and Rezz and a female drow elite warrior (Balryn) stand watch atop a ridge of natural rock in the middle of this 30-foot-high cave. Fungi, barrelstalk, and ripplebark grow in small gardens around the ridge.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see Altonrel and Rezz, along with a female drow elite warrior named Balryn, standing watch atop a ridge of natural rock in the middle of this 30-foot-high cave. Fungi, barrelstalk, and ripplebark grow in small gardens around the ridge, and stacks of supplies and gear neatly line the walls.

npcs.name: Altonrel
npcs.role: stands watch atop a ridge of natural rock in the middle of this 30-foot-high cave.
npcs.notes: [Source: Dungeon of the Mad Mage PDF]

npcs.name: Rezz
npcs.role: stands watch atop a ridge of natural rock in the middle of this 30-foot-high cave.
npcs.notes: [Source: Dungeon of the Mad Mage PDF]

npcs.name: Balryn
npcs.role: a female drow elite warrior stand watch atop a ridge of natural rock in the middle of this 30-foot-high cave.
npcs.notes: [Source: Dungeon of the Mad Mage PDF]

npcs.name: Melith
npcs.role: knows that the only route between this level of Undermountain and the next one down is the underground river.
npcs.notes: [Source: Dungeon of the Mad Mage PDF]

hazards.name: Demonweb Gate (Charisma save or pulled 5 ft toward portal)

event_triggers: On combat start: The priestess does not turn around. 'Lolth told me you were coming. She told me to make it interesting.' The gate pulses wider.
On defeat: The gate collapses with a shriek of torn reality. The candles die. In the sudden darkness, something on the other side of the closing portal whispers: 'Next time.'
On gate key found: The stairway to Level 4 unlocks. The stone grinds open like a jaw.
